Title: Subscription Order — Open Subscription

Title: Order Changes Widget — Change Log Tracks All Modifications
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with at least one prior modification
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/orders and open an existing order
2. Make a modification to the order (e.g., update customer comment)
3. Save the changes
4. Open the Changes widget on the order details blade
5. Verify the latest modification appears in the change log
6. Make a second modification (e.g., update dynamic property)
7. Save and re-open Changes widget
8. Verify both changes are tracked with timestamps and user info

Title: Order Immutability After Payment — Paid Order Items Cannot Be Edited

Admin logged in to Backend
Order exists with payment status Paid
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL}/orders
2. Open an order where the payment status is Paid
3. Attempt to modify the line item quantity in the Line Items widget
4. Attempt to add a new line item to the order
5. Attempt to remove an existing line item
6. Attempt to change the product price
7. Verify all edit controls for order items are disabled or hidden
8. Verify Save button is disabled or changes cannot be submitted

Title: Order Validation Full Field List — Complete Validation Test
Preconditions: Admin logged in to Backend (API/Swagger)
Steps:
1. Open Swagger UI at ${BACK_URL}/docs or use API client
2. Test order validation for fields: Number, CustomerId, CustomerName, StoreId, Currency, Status, CreatedDate
3. Submit invalid value for Number field (e.g., empty string or exceeding max length)
4. Submit invalid value for CustomerId (e.g., null or non-existent ID)
5. Submit invalid value for CustomerName (e.g., exceeding character limit)
6. Submit invalid value for StoreId (e.g., non-existent store)
7. Submit invalid Currency code (e.g., invalid ISO code)
8. Submit invalid Status value (e.g., non-existent status string)
9. Submit invalid CreatedDate (e.g., future date or wrong format)

Title: Order placement — inventory decremented for each line item in correct FFC
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Track inventory = TRUE
Products with known stock quantities in specific FFCs
API access available
Storefront accessible
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Inventory module
2. Record current stock quantity for Product_1 in FFC_1 (e.g., qty = 50)
3. Record current stock quantity for Product_2 in FFC_2 (e.g., qty = 30)
4. Place order via API or Storefront containing Product_1 (qty=2) and Product_2 (qty=3)
5. Verify order creation succeeds with status = New
6. Navigate to Inventory module
7. Check stock for Product_1 in FFC_1
8. Check stock for Product_2 in FFC_2
9. Verify decrements match ordered quantities

Title: Order placement — order appears in customer order history
Preconditions:
Customer account exists with known order history count
Storefront and xAPI accessible
Products available for purchase
Steps:
1. Execute xAPI query to get current order count for customer:
   query { orders(storeId: "${STORE_ID}", cultureName: "en-US", userId: "<customer-id>") { totalCount } }
2. Record current totalCount (e.g., N)
3. Place a new order via Storefront or API as the customer
4. Record the new order number
5. Execute the same xAPI orders query again
6. Verify totalCount = N + 1
7. Execute detailed order query to find the new order by number
8. Verify order details match what was placed

Title: Order placement — cart is cleared after successful order
Preconditions:
Customer account exists
Cart has items added
Storefront and xAPI accessible
Steps:
1. Add Product_1 (qty=2) and Product_2 (qty=1) to cart
2. Execute xAPI cart query to verify cart has 2 line items:
   query { cart(storeId: "${STORE_ID}", cultureName: "en-US", currencyCode: "USD") { items { quantity productId } itemsCount } }
3. Verify itemsCount = 2
4. Complete checkout and place the order
5. Verify order creation returns success with order number
6. Execute the same xAPI cart query again
7. Verify cart is empty (itemsCount = 0, items = [])

Title: Order placement — order number generated and totals match cart
Preconditions:
Admin logged in to Backend
Customer account exists
Cart has items with known prices
Number template configured in Settings > Orders
Steps:
1. Add products to cart and proceed to checkout
2. Before placing order, execute xAPI cart query to capture: cart.subTotal, cart.taxTotal, cart.shippingTotal, cart.total
3. Record cart totals
4. Place the order
5. Retrieve the new order via API: GET /api/order/customerOrders/{orderId}
6. Verify order.number is generated and follows the configured number template
7. Verify order.status = 'New'
8. Compare order.subTotal with cart.subTotal
9. Compare order.taxTotal with cart.taxTotal
10. Compare order.total with cart.total
